sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, Jacqueline, is a 30-year-old female presenting with anxiety regarding recent blood work showing a diagnosis of hepatitis C, which she was not previously aware of.
She reports a history of intravenous drug use 15 years ago, but has been clean since then.
She also reports a history of heavy alcohol use, currently consuming one beer a day, and smoking one to two cigarettes a day.
She denies any other medical conditions, but reports feeling tired at the end of the day despite getting enough sleep.
Her family history includes hypertension, diabetes, and depression.
Past medical history: The patient has a history of intravenous drug use 15 years ago, and a history of heavy alcohol use, currently consuming one beer a day.
She also smokes one to two cigarettes a day.
She has a family history of hypertension, diabetes, and depression.
Review of systems: The patient reports fatigue at the end of the day, but no other symptoms.
Current medications: [NOT MENTIONED]
Objective:
Vital signs: [NOT MENTIONED]
Physical exam: The patient appears in no distress and no jaundice on skin exam.
The heart exam reveals a regular rate and rhythm with a 2/6 systolic murmur, which has not changed from last year.
The lungs are clear with no wheezes, rales, or rhonchi.
The abdomen is soft with no hepatosplenomegaly.
The musculoskeletal exam is unremarkable with no gait disturbance or edema.
Test Results: The patient's HCV antibody test was positive, and her liver panel showed an elevated AST at 39, but normal albumin and total bilirubin levels.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient is diagnosed with hepatitis C, a viral infection that affects the liver, which she likely has had for years.
Problem list: Hepatitis C; suspected liver disease; suspected fibrosis of the liver.
Plan:
I will order additional blood work to confirm the diagnosis and determine the severity of the patient's liver disease, including checking the HCV RNA and genotype.
I will also order an ultrasound to assess fibrosis of the liver.
I will schedule the patient to follow up in three weeks to discuss the results and plan for treatment.
Follow-up: The patient will return in three weeks to discuss the results of the additional blood work and ultrasound, and to plan for treatment.
